title: Low-trust Delegated-run Preflight And Closeout Gating

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make local execution posture and selective strong-reasoning handoff cheap enough to use in ordinary work; specifically, make low-trust delegated runs safe by giving them a compact preflight contract and a posture-aware closeout gate.
intent_continuity.continuation surface: .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml lane runtime-posture-selective-handoff; linked issues #285, #286, #289 remain open

required_continuation.activation trigger: next active implementation pass on this lane; suggested_first_slice in state.toml should be updated to reflect the next unbounded gap

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: delegated runs can now receive a compact preflight answer (required checks before delegation) and a per-target closeout gate (trust level based on delegation target strength, advisory, and outcome evidence) from the workspace defaults and mixed-agent payloads.
iterative_follow_through.what it intentionally deferred: full work-semantics vocabulary from portable execplans; runtime resolution of whether a task should stay cheap/local vs. escalate to stronger reasoning vs. use external delegation; the strong-reasoning escalation handoff packet (#289); broader posture query against planning work state (#286 done boundary).
iterative_follow_through.new implications discovered: the compact summary profile (added to planning package) meaningfully reduces context cost for planning-aware agents; this should inform how summary surfaces are shaped in later slices.
iterative_follow_through.proof or validation carried forward: 210 tests passed before commit; planning surface health clean; delegated_run_guardrail queryable from agentic-workspace defaults --format json and config --format json.

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: ship the suggested_first_slice for the runtime-posture-selective-handoff lane: add low-trust delegated-run preflight and closeout gating so bounded implementation can be delegated without trusting weaker agents to interpret planning state or close lanes honestly.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: add delegated_run_guardrail to workspace mixed-agent defaults and runtime payloads; add per-target closeout_gate computed from delegation target strength, advisory, and outcome evidence; add compact summary profile to planning package CLI and installer; add focused tests.
intent_interpretation.interpretation distance: low for the first slice; the agent correctly scoped to the suggested_first_slice entry. Protocol failure was in planning hygiene, not in scope interpretation.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject closeout if the guardrail cannot be queried from defaults or config; reject if the per-target trust level does not reflect delegation target strength and advisory.

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before introducing a new runtime scheduler, a second config authority, or a broad planning-storage redesign.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: graceful-compliance work, machine-first planning-chain extraction, or strong-reasoning escalation (that belongs to #289).

stop_conditions.stop when: the slice would require designing full work-semantics resolution rather than just the preflight and closeout gate.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the guardrail surface requires consuming portable execplan fields to decide posture; that belongs to a later #286 slice.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work spreads into strong-reasoning escalation (#289) or portable execplan vocabulary changes.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: the defaults payload cannot surface the guardrail compactly or the per-target closeout_gate does not reflect the delegation target profile.

context_budget.live working set: workspace CLI defaults/config payloads, local override schema for delegation target profiles, focused planning and workspace CLI tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: full planning handoff contract, older delegation doctrine, archived lane history.
context_budget.externalize before shift: guardrail rule text, per-target trust logic, compact summary projection rules.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: delegated_run_guardrail lives in mixed_agent defaults; per-target closeout_gate is in the profile payload; compact summary strips heavy contract fields by default.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: shift after a milestone commit or when entering the next slice.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ship a first preflight and closeout gating layer for low-trust delegated runs within the runtime-posture-selective-handoff lane.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: keep the guardrail advisory and queryable; do not hard-bind to one vendor; preserve portable execplan portability; do not move lane-level issues to closed without satisfying their done boundaries.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact field names in the guardrail payload, the minimum set of preflight checks, the exact trust-classification thresholds, and the compact summary field stripping list.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the requested outcome requires designing full work-semantics resolution or hard target binding.

references: GitHub issue #285: parent lane — portable execplan semantics vs. local execution posture
GitHub issue #286: first runtime-resolution slice — preflight answer combining work semantics and local posture
GitHub issue #289: compact handoff for strong general-purpose reasoning escalation

execution_run.handoff source: uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json (compact profile not yet present at start of session)
execution_run.what happened: implemented delegated_run_guardrail in workspace mixed-agent defaults and runtime config payloads, added _delegation_target_closeout_gate helper to compute per-target trust level from delegation target profile fields, added _delegated_run_guardrail_payload to assemble the policy object, extended _mixed_agent_payload to expose closeout_gate per profile, added compact summary profile to planning package installer and CLI (stripping heavy contract sections on default JSON output), added focused test coverage across workspace CLI and planning installer tests, cleared the direct-task todo row from state.toml after code landed.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI defaults/config/mixed-agent payloads, local override schema, planning package installer and CLI, focused test files.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q (210 passed); uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py (clean); pre-commit hooks passed (all gates green)
execution_run.result for continuation: lane runtime-posture-selective-handoff remains active; issues #285, #286, #289 remain open; the delegated_run_guardrail first-slice is shipped but the broader runtime-resolution scope in #286 and the strong-reasoning escalation scope in #289 are not addressed.
execution_run.next step: archive this retroactive execplan; update suggested_first_slice in state.toml to reflect remaining work; keep issues #285, #286, #289 open.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes — the slice stayed within the suggested_first_slice boundaries
finished_run_review.intent served: partial — the slice served the first-slice intent; the lane's larger outcome remains open
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low for scope; high for planning hygiene (no execplan was created before implementation, direct-task framing was accepted without checking promotion criteria)

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --format json; pre-commit gate
proof_report.proof achieved now: agentic-workspace defaults --format json exposes mixed_agent.delegated_run_guardrail with a preflight rule, required_preflight_checks list, and closeout_gate policy including lower_trust_when conditions and lower_trust_profiles derived from live delegation target config.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: test_defaults_command_reports_machine_readable_default_routes_as_json asserts guardrail rule prefix, required_preflight_checks[0], default_trust, and lower_trust_when[0]; test_config_command_reports_local_delegation_target_profiles asserts per-target trust classification and lower_trust_profiles aggregation; 210 tests passed; all pre-commit hooks green.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: add low-trust delegated-run preflight and closeout gating so bounded implementation can be delegated without trusting weaker agents to interpret planning state or close lanes honestly.
intent_satisfaction.was original intent fully satisfied?: yes for this bounded first-slice scope
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: delegated_run_guardrail ships as a queryable contract in workspace defaults; per-target closeout_gate classifies delegation targets as normal or lower-trust based on strength, advisory, and outcome evidence; compact summary profile reduces context cost for delegated planning consumers.
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: lane runtime-posture-selective-handoff; issues #285 (parent), #286 (first runtime-resolution slice remainder), #289 (strong-reasoning escalation)

closure_check.larger-intent status: open — the runtime-posture-selective-handoff lane has three open linked issues and the lane outcome is not yet achieved
closure_check.why this decision is honest: the lane's success signal requires portable execplans to stay runtime-agnostic while local config materially affects execution, and the runtime/preflight surface can turn work semantics plus local posture into actionable guidance. This slice ships the closeout gating layer only; the posture-resolution and strong-reasoning escalation work in #286 and #289 remain unaddressed.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: commits f28f747 and 857861a, archived retroactive execplan, delegated_run_guardrail in workspace defaults and config payloads, compact summary profile in planning package.
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen if the delegated_run_guardrail contract becomes stale when further #286 slices land, or if the lower-trust classification logic needs revision based on real delegation outcome evidence.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: shipped a first preflight and closeout gating layer for low-trust delegated runs: workspace defaults now carry a delegated_run_guardrail with required preflight checks and a posture-aware closeout gate; per-target trust classification is computed from delegation target profile fields; compact summary profile reduces planning payload size for delegated consumers.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: 210 tests passed; pre-commit gate clean; planning surface health clean
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: lane runtime-posture-selective-handoff in state.toml; issues #285, #286, #289 remain open for next slice work
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: keep the delegated_run_guardrail advisory; trust classification belongs in the workspace layer, not in portable execplans; compact summary should remain the default JSON profile going forward.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (memory/docs/config): delegated_run_guardrail surfaced in workspace CLI defaults and config payloads; compact planning summary profile promoted to planning installer and CLI default.
execution_summary.resume from: read state.toml lane runtime-posture-selective-handoff and open issues #285, #286, #289; the next natural slice is the broader runtime-resolution compact answer from #286.

drift_log.note: RETROACTIVE EXECPLAN. This plan was not created before implementation. The work was incorrectly treated as a direct task throughout. The execplan was written after the commits landed as part of protocol failure remediation. See the protocol failure analysis committed alongside this plan.
